title: Four-Bar Linkage
goal: A four-bar linkage is one of the fundamental mechanisms in mechanical engineering. It converts rotational motion into complex curved trajectories, enabling versatile motion patterns with simple construction.
parts: Ground Link: Fixed base connecting two pivot points
Input Link (Crank): Rotating driver link providing input motion
Coupler: Floating link connecting input and output
Output Link (Rocker): Driven link producing output motion
advantages: Reliable motion transmission with minimal wear
Wide range of achievable motion paths
disadvantages: Output motion depends on all link ratios
Difficult to achieve precise straight-line motion
materials: Metal bars or rods for structural links
Precision bearings or bushings for pivot joints
cautions: Verify clearances to avoid link collisions
Check for full rotation capability before assembly
Ensure proper joint lubrication for longevity
Consider mechanical advantage at extreme positions
